name: Exceptions
description: Questions on try/except and exception handling.

questions.question: After an exception is raised and caught, execution:
questions.explanation: After handling the exception, execution continues with the code after the try/except.

questions.question: except Exception as e: catches:
choices: All exceptions (except BaseException subclasses like SystemExit)
questions.explanation: Exception is the base for most built-in exceptions; catching it catches most errors.

questions.question: raise ValueError('bad') does what?
choices: Creates and raises a ValueError with message 'bad'
questions.explanation: raise creates an exception instance and raises it; the string is the exception message.

questions.question: try: ... finally: ... ensures:
choices: The finally block runs (even after return or exception)
questions.explanation: finally runs whether the try raises or not, and even after return in try.

questions.question: Catching BaseException is generally:
choices: Avoided; it catches SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt
questions.explanation: BaseException includes SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt; catching it can prevent normal exit.
